name: Quick Kimchi (Korean Mak Kimchi)
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the leaves of a Chinese cabbage and cut them into 2-inch lengths (the leafy end may be left longer; it will shrink the most). Peel the Chinese turnip, halve it lengthwise down the center, and then slice thin across the grain.
2. Combine the salt and water in a large pot. Place the vegetables in the pot, pouring the salted water over them so they are completely covered. Cover the pot and leave overnight.
3. The next day, mince the scallions, garlic, and ginger. Drain the wilted vegetables, reserving the salty liquid. Season with the cayenne pepper, adding the minced scallions, garlic, and ginger. Mix the vegetables by hand, using rubber gloves if available. Pack the mixture tightly into jars, pouring the reserved salted water over it so that the liquid level is within 1/2 inch of the top of the jar.
4. Store at room temperature for 2 or 3 days before refrigerating. This recipe makes about 2 jars of kimchi suitable for a large dinner party.
